content: Global preference: Indentation should be 2 spaces across projects unless otherwise specified.
Writing guideline: For emails and docs, subject lines should be ≤50 characters.
When listing action items, use bullet lists, not prose paragraphs.
In JavaScript projects, prefer pnpm over npm for package management.
Correction: Your code block used 4-space indentation. Use 2 spaces globally.
Project acme-site: always use Tailwind CSS, not Bootstrap.
Correction (writing): The subject line was too long. Keep subject lines ≤50 characters.
Correction (code): You used npm in the example. Please use pnpm by default.
Correction (acme-site): You added Bootstrap classes. For acme-site we use Tailwind CSS.
Correction: Convert the action items into bullet points.
Reminder: 2-space indentation is our global standard.
Acknowledged. I will apply 2-space indentation globally unless otherwise specified.
Yes, always — re: 2-space indentation globally.
I accidentally pasted a test API key: sk-ABC123. Do not store credentials in memory. Please redact and forget this.
Project acme-site only: indent with 4 spaces to match the existing codebase. This overrides the global 2-space rule.
Let's enforce the writing rule: subject lines ≤50 chars.
Confirm — re: subject lines ≤50 characters in the writing domain.
Reminder for acme-site: use 4-space indentation.
acme-site: Tailwind only. No Bootstrap.
Yes, always — re: Tailwind-only standard for acme-site.
Please always use bullet lists for action items rather than long paragraphs.
Yes, always — re: bullets for action items.
For acme-site, use 4 spaces (override global).
Confirm — re: acme-site indentation override to 4 spaces.
Default to pnpm for JS package management across code examples.
Confirm — re: pnpm over npm (code domain).
Drafted the email with a 62-character subject line. Let me know if you want changes.
Correction: subject line must be ≤50 characters. Please shorten it.
